title.en: HTTP Fundamentals: The Language of the Web

description.en: Understand how browsers and servers communicate using HTTP methods, status codes, and headers.
description.ta: HTTP முறைகள், நிலை குறியீடுகள் மற்றும் தலைப்புகளைப் பயன்படுத்தி உலாவிகளும் சேவையகங்களும் எவ்வாறு தொடர்பு கொள்கின்றன என்பதைப் புரிந்துகொள்ளுங்கள்.

story_variants.en:
## The Post Office of the Internet

Vaathiyaar walks into class carrying a stack of envelopes. "Every time you visit a website, your browser sends a **letter** to a server and waits for a **reply**. That letter follows strict rules — those rules are **HTTP**."

### What is HTTP?
- **H**yper**T**ext **T**ransfer **P**rotocol
- A request-response protocol between client and server
- Stateless: each request is independent
- Uses methods (verbs) to describe the action

### HTTP Methods

| Method | Purpose | Example |
|--------|---------|----------|
| `GET` | Retrieve data | Load a webpage |
| `POST` | Send data | Submit a form |
| `PUT` | Replace data | Update a profile |
| `PATCH` | Partial update | Change just the email |
| `DELETE` | Remove data | Delete an account |

### Status Codes

| Range | Meaning | Common Codes |
|-------|---------|---------------|
| `2xx` | Success | 200 OK, 201 Created |
| `3xx` | Redirect | 301 Moved, 304 Not Modified |
| `4xx` | Client Error | 400 Bad Request, 404 Not Found |
| `5xx` | Server Error | 500 Internal Server Error |

### Headers
Headers carry metadata: `Content-Type`, `Authorization`, `User-Agent`, `Accept`.

> **Key Concept:** HTTP is the foundation of ALL web communication. Every API call, every webpage load, every form submission uses HTTP.

explanation.en: Import the requests library for making HTTP calls
Send a GET request to GitHub's API endpoint
Check the HTTP status code — 200 means success
Read the Content-Type header to see the response format
Parse the response body as JSON into a Python dict
Confirm the parsed data is a Python dictionary
Access a specific key from the JSON response
Now let's see how to send data WITH a request
Create a dictionary with the data to send
Send a POST request with JSON payload in the body
httpbin echoes back our data with a 200 status
The server echoes back the JSON we sent

animation_sequence.left_content:
GET /api/users HTTP/1.1
Host: example.com
Accept: application/json
Authorization: Bearer token123

instruction.en: Use the requests library to make a GET request to 'https://httpbin.org/get'. Print the status code, the Content-Type header, and the 'origin' field from the JSON response.
instruction.ta: 'https://httpbin.org/get' என்ற முகவரிக்கு GET கோரிக்கை அனுப்புங்கள். நிலை குறியீடு, Content-Type தலைப்பு மற்றும் JSON பதிலிலிருந்து 'origin' புலத்தை அச்சிடுங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
import requests

# Make a GET request to httpbin
response = requests.get('https://httpbin.org/get')

# Print the status code
print(f'Status: {response.status_code}')

# Print the Content-Type header
print(f'Content-Type: {response.headers["Content-Type"]}')

# Parse JSON and print the origin IP
data = response.json()
print(f'Origin: {data["origin"]}')

en: Use requests.get(url) to make a GET request
Access status_code attribute for the HTTP status
Call response.json() to parse the body as a dict
ta: உடலை dict ஆக பாகுபடுத்த response.json() அழையுங்கள்

q.en: Which HTTP method is used to retrieve data from a server?
q.ta: சேவையகத்திலிருந்து தரவைப் பெற எந்த HTTP முறை பயன்படுத்தப்படுகிறது?

q.en: What does a 404 status code mean?
Which header tells the server what format of data the client accepts?
q.ta: கிளையன்ட் எந்த வடிவ தரவை ஏற்றுக்கொள்கிறது என்று சேவையகத்திற்கு எந்த தலைப்பு தெரிவிக்கிறது?
